intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: The user prefers clean, data-forward visual structures over narrative or decorative elements
facade.label: Minimalist grid
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward structured tables, charts, and card-based layouts; reduce illustration and ambient design
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer warmer, more organic visual language—hand-drawn elements, soft curves, or narrative framing around data

evidence.hypothesis: The user prefers clean, data-forward visual structures over narrative or decorative elements
evidence.implication: Lean toward structured tables, charts, and card-based layouts; reduce illustration and ambient design

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer warm, approachable language that feels like talking to a helpful friend rather than reading medical documentation
facade.label: Friendly and conversational
facade.acceptImplies: The voice should use casual language, encouragement, and personality—e.g., 'Nice work this week!' instead of 'Data logged successfully'
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer a more neutral, clinical, or straightforward tone that doesn't inject personality into interactions

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, eased animations over instant state changes when tracking health data over time
facade.label: Smooth sliding transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard updates (metrics, charts, logs) should ease-in/ease-out rather than pop instantly; values animate when they shift
facade.rejectImplies: Instant updates are acceptable; animation overhead is wasteful for a data dashboard

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer prominent metric displays (one KPI per card) over narrative summaries for tracking health experiments
facade.axisTargeted: Data density and visual hierarchy—whether to lead with headline numbers or wrap metrics in explanatory text
facade.label: Big number, small context
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should prioritize large, scannable numbers; context lives in secondary text or tooltips
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should pair every metric with its narrative—why it matters, what changed, what's next—making text and number equally weighted

facade.hypothesis: A fixed left sidebar with collapsible experiment categories, paired with spacious card layouts for each tracked metric
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs) + content density (cards vs lists)
facade.label: Sidebar + Cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers persistent, scannable navigation with generous whitespace around individual experiments
facade.rejectImplies: User may favor persistent top navigation, denser list views, or tab-based switching between experiments

facade.hypothesis: Health dashboards feel more trustworthy and less clinical when using soft, desaturated colors (warm beiges, soft grays, muted teals) rather than bright primary colors or stark whites.
facade.label: Warm muted tones
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers calming, organic color palettes that feel personal rather than medical/sterile. Expect preference for soft typography, gentle gradients, and photos over flat icons.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer brighter, higher-contrast interfaces, or clinical clarity. Could indicate taste for bold accent colors, stark layouts, or more 'official' dashboard aesthetics.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: The user wants numerical insights presented with human-friendly warmth rather than cold, clinical precision—suggesting they value emotional connection to their health data
facade.label: Warm data viz
facade.acceptImplies: Favor warmer color palettes, rounded elements, and softer typography in charts and stats; create a sense of personal care rather than medical sterility
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward cooler tones, sharp lines, and technical precision; the user wants data to feel objective and neutral, not emotionally guided
